Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple A basic-level cyber attack targeting water and wastewater systems begins with phishing attempts against internet-facing assets, progresses to executing malicious files through deceptive means, and culminates in data manipulation impacting operational processes.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Employee endpoints, Operational Technology (OT) devices

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send phishing emails with malicious attachments or links to employees, aiming to trick them into downloading malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-635: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: Upon opening the malicious attachment/link, the reg.exe utility is exploited to execute the embedded malware, allowing the attackers to establish control over the system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers manipulate system configurations or critical data within the OS process to disrupt operations, affecting the functionality of water and wastewater systems.

Scenario.summary: This simplistic cyber attack leverages phishing to gain entry into the targeted water and wastewater systems, followed by execution of malicious payloads, ultimately leading to impactful data manipulation, potentially causing operational disruptions.
